question_text: Which of the following is the correct definition of wavelength?

options.label: A
options.text: The number of waves produced in one second

options.label: B
options.text: The distance between one point on a wave and the same point on the next wave

options.label: C
options.text: The maximum distance a wave moves from its starting position

options.label: D
options.text: The speed at which a wave travels

correct_answer: The distance between one point on a wave and the same point on the next wave
explanation: Wavelength is the distance between two identical points on consecutive waves, such as from crest to crest or trough to trough. It is measured in metres. Option A describes frequency, Option C describes amplitude, and Option D describes wave speed.
